info.name: CorperCompass API
info.description: Complete API for CorperCompass relocation platform. Includes authentication, areas, lodges, checklist, cultural content, budget estimator, map markers, messaging, and negotiations.

request.description:
Creates a new user account in the CorperCompass system.  
New users are assigned the "user" role by default.  
A profile document is automatically created alongside  
the user account.

Requirements:

- Public endpoint (no authentication required)
    

Request Body:

- name (string, required) - Full name of the user  
    Example: "Richard Ilori"
    
- email (string, required) - Valid email address  
    Example: "[richard@test.com](https://mailto:richard@test.com)"
    
- password (string, required) - Minimum 6 characters  
    Example: "password123"
    

Success Response (201):

- _id - MongoDB generated user ID
    
- name - User's full name
    
- email - User's email address
    
- role - Default is "user"
    
- Sets JWT token in HTTP-only cookie
    

Error Responses:

- 400 - User already exists
    
- 400 - Invalid user data
    
- invalid email format, password too short)

Authenticates an existing user and returns a JWT token.  
The token is required to access all protected endpoints.  
Store the token and include it as a Bearer token in the  
Authorization header for subsequent requests.

Requirements:

- Public endpoint (no authentication required)
    

Request Body:

- email (string, required) - Registered email address  
    Example: "[richard@test.com](https://mailto:richard@test.com)"
    
- password (string, required) - User's password  
    Example: "password123"
    

Success Response (200):

- _id - User's MongoDB ID
    
- name - User's full name
    
- email - User's email address
    
- role - "user" or "admin"
    
- token - JWT token for authenticating future requests
    
- Sets JWT token in HTTP-only cookie
    

Error Responses:

- 400 - Missing email or password
    
- 400 - Invalid email format
    
- 401 - Invalid email or password

Returns the account details of the currently  
authenticated user. Use this to verify a valid  
session or retrieve basic user information without  
exposing sensitive data like passwords.

Requirements:

- Must be logged in (Bearer token required)
    
- Add token to Authorization header as Bearer token
    

Request Body:

- None (GET request)
    

Success Response (200):

- _id - User's MongoDB ID
    
- name - User's full name
    
- email - User's email address
    
- role - "user" or "admin"
    
- createdAt - Account creation timestamp
    
- updatedAt - Last update timestamp
    

Error Responses:

- 401 - Not authorized, no token
    
- 401 - Not authorized, token failed

Returns the relocation profile of the currently  
authenticated user. The profile contains NYSC-specific  
information like posted state, phone number and  
preferences. A profile is automatically created when  
a user registers.

Requirements:

- Must be logged in (Bearer token required)
    
- Add token to Authorization header as Bearer token
    

Request Body:

- None (GET request)
    

Success Response (200):

- _id - Profile MongoDB ID
    
- user - Reference to the User ID
    
- postedState - NYSC posted state e.g. "Enugu"
    
- phone - User's phone number
    
- preferences - User preferences object  
    e.g. { "budget": "low" }
    
- createdAt - Profile creation timestamp
    
- updatedAt - Last update timestamp
    

Error Responses:

- 401 - Not authorized, no token
    
- 404 - Profile not found

Updates the relocation profile of the currently  
authenticated user. Use this to set your NYSC posted  
state, contact number and budget preferences.  
All fields are optional — only include what you want  
to update.

Requirements:

- Must be logged in (Bearer token required)
    
- Add token to Authorization header as Bearer token
    

Request Body (all fields optional):

- postedState (string) - NYSC posted state  
    Example: "Enugu"
    
- phone (string) - Contact phone number  
    Example: "08087654321"
    
- preferences (object) - Budget and other preferences  
    Example: { "budget": "low" }
    

Success Response (200):

- Returns the fully updated profile document
    

Error Responses:

- 401 - Not authorized, no token
    
- 404 - Profile not found

Returns a list of all areas in the CorperCompass database.  
Areas contain location intelligence including safety ratings,  
rent estimates, and lifestyle notes to help corpers make  
informed relocation decisions.

Requirements:

- Public endpoint (no authentication required)
    

Query Parameters (optional):

- state (string) - Filter by state name  
    Example: /api/areas?state=Enugu
    

Success Response (200):

- Returns array of area documents
    
- Each area contains name, state, safety info, rent range
    
- Returns empty array \[\] if no areas exist yet
    

Error Responses:

- 500 - Server error

Returns a single area by its MongoDB ID.  
Use this to get detailed information about a  
specific area including safety ratings, rent range,  
and lifestyle notes.

Requirements:

- Public endpoint (no authentication required)
    

Path Variables:

- id (string, required) - Valid MongoDB ObjectId  
    Example: 69bb3cef9d88c4178257716f
    

Success Response (200):

- Returns the full area document
    

Error Responses:

- 400 - Invalid area ID format
    
- 404 - Area not found

Creates a new area. Admin access required.  
Areas provide location intelligence for corpers  
including rent ranges, transport and safety notes.  
Only create areas for the 3 pilot states:  
Enugu, Oyo and Kaduna.

Requirements:

- Must be logged in as Admin (Bearer token required)
    

Request Body:

- name (string, required) - Area name e.g. "GRA"
    
- state (string, required) - Pilot state only  
    e.g. "Enugu", "Oyo", "Kaduna"
    
- rentRange (object) - Min and max monthly rent  
    e.g. { "min": 150000, "max": 400000 }
    
- transportNotes (string) - Transport information
    
- safetyNotes (string) - Safety information
    
- lifestyleNotes (string) - Lifestyle information
    
- location.coordinates (array) - \[longitude, latitude\]
    

Success Response (201):

- Returns the created area document
    

Error Responses:

- 401 - Not authorized, no token
    
- 403 - Not authorized as admin

Updates an existing area by its MongoDB ID.  
Admin access required. Only include the fields  
you want to update — all fields are optional.

Requirements:

- Must be logged in as Admin (Bearer token required)
    

Path Variables:

- id (string, required) - Valid MongoDB ObjectId  
    of the area to update
    

Request Body (all fields optional):

- name (string) - Area name
    
- state (string) - Pilot state
    
- rentRange (object) - { "min": number, "max": number }
    
- transportNotes (string) - Transport information
    
- safetyNotes (string) - Safety information
    
- lifestyleNotes (string) - Lifestyle information
    
- location.coordinates (array) - \[longitude, latitude\]
    

Success Response (200):

- Returns the fully updated area document
    

Error Responses:

- 400 - Invalid area ID format
    
- 401 - Not authorized, no token
    
- 403 - Not authorized as admin
    
- 404 - Area not found

Permanently deletes an area by its MongoDB ID.  
Admin access required.  
Note: This is a hard delete — the area is completely  
removed from the database and cannot be recovered.  
Compare with Lodge deletion which uses soft delete.

Requirements:

- Must be logged in as Admin (Bearer token required)
    

Path Variables:

- id (string, required) - Valid MongoDB ObjectId  
    of the area to delete
    

Request Body:

- None
    

Success Response (200):

- { "message": "Area removed" }
    

Error Responses:

- 400 - Invalid area ID format
    
- 401 - Not authorized, no token
    
- 403 - Not authorized as admin
    
- 404 - Area not found

Retrieves a list of all active lodges in the directory.  
By default, it returns all lodges. You can optionally filter by a specific Area ID.

Requirements:

- Public endpoint (no authentication required)
    

Query Parameters (optional):

- area (string) - Filter lodges by a valid Area MongoDB ObjectId  
    Example: /api/lodges?area=69bc78c85c88be8e531110c3
    

Success Response (200):

- Returns an array of active lodge documents
    
- Automatically populates the 'area' field with the area's name and state
    
- Only returns lodges where isActive is true
    
- Returns an empty array \[\] if no lodges exist yet
    

Error Responses:

- 400 - Invalid area ID format in query parameter

Retrieves the details of a single lodge by its MongoDB ID.  
Includes populated Area data (name and state).

Requirements:

- Public endpoint (no authentication required)
    

Path Variables:

- id (string, required) - Valid MongoDB ObjectId of the lodge  
    Example: "69bc7a2caf6128a6699b1dc9"
    

Success Response (200):

- Returns the full lodge document
    
- Automatically populates the 'area' field
    

Error Responses:

- 400 - Invalid lodge ID format
    
- 404 - Lodge not found

Creates a new lodge in the directory. Admin access required.  
Lodges must be linked to a valid Area ID.

Requirements:

- Must be logged in as Admin (Bearer token required)
    

Request Body:

- name (string, required) - Name of the lodge  
    Example: "Sunshine Lodge"
    
- area (string, required) - Valid MongoDB ObjectId of the parent Area  
    Example: "69bc78c85c88be8e531110c3"
    
- priceRange (object) - Min and max rent  
    Example: { "min": 150000, "max": 250000 }
    
- publicContact (string) - Phone number for caretaker  
    Example: "08012345678"
    
- notes (string) - Additional descriptions  
    Example: "Close to market"
    
- location.coordinates (array) - \[longitude, latitude\]  
    Example: \[3.3792, 6.5244\]
    

Success Response (201):

- Returns the fully created lodge document
    
- Automatically sets isActive to true
    

Error Responses:

- 400 - Missing required fields or invalid Area ID format
    
- 401 - Not authorized, no token
    
- 403 - Not authorized as admin

Updates an existing lodge by its MongoDB ID.  
Admin access required. Only include the fields  
you want to update — all fields are optional.

Requirements:

- Must be logged in as Admin (Bearer token required)
    

Path Variables:

- id (string, required) - Valid MongoDB ObjectId  
    of the lodge to update
    

Request Body (all fields optional):

- name (string) - Lodge name
    
- price (number) - Monthly rent price
    
- roomType (string) - "self-contain", "room-and-parlour",  
    "flat" or "duplex"
    
- amenities (array) - List of amenities  
    e.g. \["water", "electricity", "security"\]
    
- publicContact (string) - Contact phone number
    
- notes (string) - Additional notes
    
- isActive (boolean) - Whether lodge is active
    

Success Response (200):

- Returns the fully updated lodge document
    

Error Responses:

- 400 - Invalid lodge ID format
    
- 401 - Not authorized, no token
    
- 403 - Not authorized as admin
    
- 404 - Lodge not found

Soft deletes a lodge by setting isDeleted to true.  
Admin access required. The lodge remains in the  
database but is hidden from all public endpoints.  
This preserves data for accountability purposes.

Requirements:

- Must be logged in as Admin (Bearer token required)
    

Path Variables:

- id (string, required) - Valid MongoDB ObjectId
    

Request Body:

- None
    

Success Response (200):

- { "message": "Lodge successfully archived" }
    

Error Responses:

- 400 - Invalid lodge ID format
    
- 401 - Not authorized, no token
    
- 403 - Not authorized as admin
    
- 404 - Lodge not found

Returns all journey sections and their checklist  
items to guide corpers through the relocation process.  
Sections include pre-arrival, arrival and settlement tasks.

Requirements:

- Must be logged in (Bearer token required)
    

Request Body:

- None (GET request)
    

Success Response (200):

- Returns array of journey section documents
    
- Empty array \[\] if no sections seeded yet
    

Error Responses:

- 401 - Not authorized, no token

Returns the authenticated user's checklist progress.  
Shows which tasks have been completed and which  
are still pending.

Requirements:

- Must be logged in (Bearer token required)
    

Request Body:

- None (GET request)
    

Success Response (200):

- Returns array of user checklist progress documents
    
- Empty array \[\] if no progress recorded yet
    

Error Responses:

- 401 - Not authorized, no token

Toggles the completion status of a checklist item  
for the authenticated user. If no progress record  
exists it creates one. If it exists it updates it.

Requirements:

- Must be logged in (Bearer token required)
    

Path Variables:

- itemId (string, required) - Valid MongoDB ObjectId  
    of the checklist item to toggle
    

Request Body:

- completed (boolean, required) - true or false  
    Example: { "completed": true }
    

Success Response (200):

- Returns the updated progress document with  
    completedAt timestamp if completed is true
    

Error Responses:

- 400 - Invalid checklist item ID format
    
- 401 - Not authorized, no token

Returns cultural content filtered by category and state.  
Helps corpers learn local traditions, language and customs.

Requirements:

- Public endpoint (no authentication required)
    

Query Parameters (optional):

- category (string) - Filter by category  
    e.g. "Festivals", "Language", "Food"
    
- state (string) - Filter by pilot state  
    e.g. "Enugu", "Oyo", "Kaduna"
    

Success Response (200):

- Returns array of cultural content documents
    
- Empty array \[\] if no content seeded yet
    

Error Responses:

- 500 - Server error

Returns a single cultural content document by ID.

Requirements:

- Public endpoint (no authentication required)
    

Path Variables:

- id (string, required) - Valid MongoDB ObjectId
    

Success Response (200):

- Returns the full cultural content document
    

Error Responses:

- 400 - Invalid content ID format
    
- 404 - Content not found

StartFragmentCalculates estimated monthly budget for a corper  
including a 15% buffer for unexpected expenses.  
Triggers a risk alert if total is below threshold.  
  
Requirements:  
\- Public endpoint (no authentication required)  
  
Request Body:  
\- rent (number) - Monthly rent  
\- feeding (number) - Monthly feeding cost  
\- transport (number) - Monthly transport cost  
\- miscellaneous (number) - Other monthly expenses  
  
Success Response (200):  
\- total - Sum of all expenses  
\- buffer - 15% of total for emergencies  
\- recommendedBudget - total + buffer  
\- riskAlert - true if budget is dangerously low  
  
Error Responses:  
\- 400 - Invalid input dataEndFragment

Returns all areas and active lodges with coordinates  
for display as markers on the interactive map.  
Soft deleted lodges are excluded automatically.

Requirements:

- Public endpoint (no authentication required)
    

Request Body:

- None (GET request)
    

Success Response (200):

- Returns array of marker objects
    
- Each marker has type "area" or "lodge"
    
- Includes coordinates, name, state and price info
    

Error Responses:

- 500 - Server error

Returns weighted coordinate points for generating  
a rent price heatmap overlay on the map.  
Higher weight means higher rent in that area.  
Soft deleted lodges are excluded automatically.

Requirements:

- Public endpoint (no authentication required)
    

Request Body:

- None (GET request)
    

Success Response (200):

- Returns array of { lat, lng, weight } objects
    
- Weight is derived from lodge price
    

Error Responses:

- 500 - Server error

Retrieves a list of all conversations for the authenticated user.  
Sorted by most recent first.

Requirements:

- Must be logged in (Bearer token required)
    

Request Body:

- None (GET request)
    

Success Response (200):

- Returns an array of message documents
    
- Automatically populates sender and receiver details (name, email)
    
- Returns empty array \[\] if no messages exist
    

Error Responses:

- 401 - Not authorized, no token

Sends a new message to another user.  
Users cannot send messages to themselves.

Requirements:

- Must be logged in (Bearer token required)
    

Request Body:

- receiver (string, required) - Valid MongoDB ObjectId of the recipient  
    Example: "69bb3cef9d88c4178257716f"
    
- content (string, required) - The message text  
    Example: "Is this lodge still available?"
    

Success Response (201):

- Returns the created message document
    
- Automatically populates sender and receiver details
    

Error Responses:

- 400 - Missing fields, invalid ID format, or attempting to message oneself
    
- 401 - Not authorized, no token

Retrieves the complete message history between the authenticated user and a specific user.  
Sorted chronologically (oldest to newest).

Requirements:

- Must be logged in (Bearer token required)
    

Path Variables:

- userId (string, required) - Valid MongoDB ObjectId of the other user
    

Success Response (200):

- Returns an array of message documents
    
- Returns empty array \[\] if no conversation exists yet
    

Error Responses:

- 400 - Invalid user ID format
    
- 401 - Not authorized, no token

Marks a specific message as read.  
Only the designated receiver of the message can mark it as read.

Requirements:

- Must be logged in (Bearer token required)
    

Path Variables:

- id (string, required) - Valid MongoDB ObjectId of the message
    

Success Response (200):

- Returns the updated message document with read: true
    

Error Responses:

- 400 - Invalid message ID format
    
- 401 - Not authorized, no token
    
- 403 - Not authorized (user is not the receiver)
    
- 404 - Message not found

Retrieves a list of all rent negotiations for the authenticated user.  
Sorted by the most recently updated.

Requirements:

- Must be logged in (Bearer token required)
    

Request Body:

- None (GET request)
    

Success Response (200):

- Returns an array of negotiation documents
    
- Automatically populates the 'lodge' details (name, area, price)
    
- Returns an empty array \[\] if the user has no active negotiations
    

Error Responses:

- 401 - Not authorized, no token

Starts a new rent negotiation for a specific lodge.  
Creates the initial offer and an optional first message.

Requirements:

- Must be logged in (Bearer token required)
    

Request Body:

- lodge (string, required) - Valid MongoDB ObjectId of the lodge
    
- proposedPrice (number, required) - The initial rent offer
    
- message (string) - Optional first message to the landlord/admin
    

Success Response (201):

- Returns the created negotiation document
    
- Status automatically defaults to "pending"
    

Error Responses:

- 400 - Missing required fields (lodge, proposedPrice)
    
- 401 - Not authorized, no token
    
- 404 - Lodge not found

Retrieves the complete details of a single rent negotiation.  
Includes the full message history and populated lodge details.

Requirements:

- Must be logged in (Bearer token required)
    
- Users can only view their own negotiations (unless Admin)
    

Path Variables:

- id (string, required) - Valid MongoDB ObjectId of the negotiation
    

Success Response (200):

- Returns the full negotiation document
    
- Automatically populates the 'lodge' and 'messages.sender' fields
    

Error Responses:

- 400 - Invalid negotiation ID format
    
- 401 - Not authorized, no token
    
- 403 - Not authorized (user is not part of this negotiation)
    
- 404 - Negotiation not found

Updates the status of a specific rent negotiation.  
Used by admins/landlords to accept or reject, or by users to cancel.

Requirements:

- Must be logged in (Bearer token required)
    
- Must be a participant in the negotiation or an Admin
    

Path Variables:

- id (string, required) - Valid MongoDB ObjectId of the negotiation
    

Request Body:

- status (string, required) - The new status (e.g., "accepted", "rejected", "cancelled")  
    Example: { "status": "accepted" }
    

Success Response (200):

- Returns the updated negotiation document
    

Error Responses:

- 400 - Invalid negotiation ID format or invalid status
    
- 401 - Not authorized, no token
    
- 403 - Not authorized to update this negotiation
    
- 404 - Negotiation not found

Adds a new message to an existing rent negotiation.  
Automatically routes the message to the other participant (corper to landlord, or landlord to corper).

Requirements:

- Must be logged in (Bearer token required)
    
- Must be an active participant in the negotiation (or an Admin)
    

Path Variables:

- id (string, required) - Valid MongoDB ObjectId of the negotiation
    

Request Body:

- content (string, required) - The text of the message  
    Example: { "content": "Thank you for accepting my offer! How do I pay?" }
    

Success Response (201):

- Returns the newly created message document
    
- Automatically populates the sender and receiver details (name, email)
    

Error Responses:

- 400 - Missing message content or invalid negotiation ID format
    
- 401 - Not authorized, no token
    
- 403 - Not authorized (user is not part of this negotiation)
    
- 404 - Negotiation not found
